F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When arriving at a host’s home or event, the guest waits for the host to indicate where to sit instead of choosing a seat independently.

GPT-5.2.name: Dimas Pratama
GPT-5.2.story:
Dimas arrived at Rina’s house in Depok just as other guests were slipping off their sandals at the door. He greeted Rina’s mother with a small nod and a "Excuse me, ma’am," then stepped inside, holding a box of kue lapis with both hands. The living room had a low coffee table, several chairs, and a long sofa already half filled.

Rina waved from near the kitchen. "Dimas, come in. Please," she said, then turned to her aunt. Dimas stayed by the edge of the rug, smiling, his hands clasped in front of him.

A cousin patted an empty chair. "Just sit here," he offered.

Dimas glanced toward Rina’s mother instead of moving. "Where would be best, ma’am?" he asked softly.

Rina’s mother looked up from arranging glasses. "Oh, Dimas, sit on the sofa at the far end, near the fan so it is cool," she said.

"All right, ma’am," Dimas replied. He walked over without shifting any furniture, sat where she indicated, and placed the cake on the side table. Rina came by with iced tea and set it in front of him.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has a mobility need, the guest politely explains the constraint and asks the host which seat would be most appropriate before sitting down.

GPT-5.2.name: Sari Wulandari
GPT-5.2.story:
Sari arrived at her colleague Andi’s apartment in Surabaya for a small arisan. She slipped off her shoes, adjusted the strap of her tote, and waited near the doorway while greetings moved around the room. The floor seating area was already arranged with cushions and a low table covered in snacks.

Andi smiled and stepped toward her. "Sari, come in. Just sit on the carpet," he said, gesturing to the cushions.

Sari returned the smile and held up her hand gently. "Can I say something first, Di? My knee is acting up, so it is a bit hard to sit low and stand again," she said.

Andi’s expression shifted to attention. "Oh, yes, yes. A chair would be better for you," he replied, looking around.

Sari stayed standing. "Where would be most appropriate? I do not want to disrupt the flow of the event," she asked.

Andi pointed to a chair near the wall, close to the fan and with space to stand. "Just sit here. It will also be easy if you want to get food later," he said.

"Thanks, Di," Sari replied. She walked over carefully, sat down slowly, and placed her tote beside the chair. Andi brought a glass of water and set it on the small side table next to her.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest waits for the host’s seating cue, but then complains about the assigned seat in a way that makes the host feel disregarded.

GPT-5.2.name: Arief Nugroho
GPT-5.2.story:
Arief arrived at Nanda’s rented house in Bandung for a birthday gathering and stood by the entrance as people chatted. He smiled at Nanda’s father and kept his hands behind his back, waiting while Nanda finished greeting another guest.

Nanda turned and pointed across the room. "Arief, sit in the chair near the window," she said.

"Okay," Arief replied, walking over and sitting where she indicated. He glanced at the window, then leaned forward to look around the room.

A moment later, Nanda came with a plate of pastel. "Here, try one first," she said, placing it on the coffee table.

Arief took one, then sighed audibly. "Wow, this seat is right in the wind from the window. It is really cold here," he said, rubbing his forearms.

Nanda paused with her hand still hovering over the plate. "Oh, do you want to move?" she asked.

Arief tilted his head toward the sofa. "I think it would be more comfortable over there. This chair is hard too," he continued, pressing the seat with his palm.

Nanda looked toward the sofa where older relatives were sitting, then back at Arief. She reached for the window latch and started to close it a little while Arief watched.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When arriving at a host’s home or event, the guest picks a seat immediately, rearranges chairs, and ignores the host’s guidance.

GPT-5.2.name: Kevin Hartono
GPT-5.2.story:
Kevin stepped into the house in Tangerang with his phone still in his hand, scanning the living room like it was a café. Without pausing, he walked straight to the sofa and dropped his backpack on it. Two people were mid-conversation there; they shifted their knees to make space.

Rani, the host, came out from the hallway. "Kevin, just sit here near the dining table, so it will be easy when you want to eat," she said, pointing to a chair near the dining area.

Kevin didn’t look over. "Ah, it is better here," he replied, already leaning back. He tugged the sofa cushion, then stood up and dragged a chair from the corner so he could stretch his legs onto it. The chair legs scraped the tile, and a few heads turned.

Rani walked closer, hand half raised as if to stop him. "That chair is for Uncle Harun later," she said.

Kevin shrugged and slid the chair tighter to the sofa anyway. "He can take another one later," he said. He reached across the coffee table to pull it closer, nudging someone’s glass, and the drink sloshed as the person steadied it with both hands.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: To communicate warmth, the visitor clearly shows they enjoy the other person’s company by giving friendly verbal appreciation and attentive engagement.

GPT-5.2.name: Rizky Maulana
GPT-5.2.story:
Rizky visited his old classmate Sinta in Malang on a Saturday afternoon. He arrived with a small bag of gifts from the bakery near the station and handed it over at the doorway. "I brought pull-apart bread, I hope it suits your taste," he said.

Inside, Sinta poured tea and started telling him about her new job. Rizky sat upright on the sofa, phone face-down on the table, and looked at her as she spoke.

"So now you handle out-of-town clients?" he asked, waiting until she finished a sentence.

"Yes, often through online meetings," Sinta replied.

Rizky nodded and smiled. "That sounds interesting. I am glad I can hear your story directly. It has been so long since we talked like this," he said, picking up a photo frame from the shelf only after glancing at Sinta for permission with his eyes.

Sinta laughed. "That is a photo from graduation."

"Wow, I remember that day," Rizky said. "Thank you for inviting me over. Your home is really comfortable." He set the frame back carefully and leaned in as Sinta continued.

When Sinta’s mother walked by, Rizky stood and greeted her, then returned to his seat, listening with his hands folded on his knees.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor is shy or tired, the visitor still expresses appreciation directly at least once and uses attentive listening to show goodwill.

GPT-5.2.name: Lina Kusuma
GPT-5.2.story:
Lina arrived at her aunt’s house in Denpasar after a long day of traveling. Her shoulders sagged as she stepped inside, and she rubbed her eyes briefly. Her aunt, Bu Rini, greeted her with a hug. "Tired, dear?" she asked.

"Yes, Auntie, a little," Lina replied with a small smile. She placed her bag neatly by the wall and sat when her aunt gestured to a chair.

Bu Rini started chatting about the neighborhood. Lina didn’t talk much, but she faced her aunt and nodded at the right moments. When her aunt mentioned a new food stall nearby, Lina lifted her head.

"Oh, the one that sells sate lilit?" Lina asked, voice quiet.

"Yes, it is good," Bu Rini said.

Lina listened, hands resting on her lap, and kept her phone in her bag. When Bu Rini brought a bowl of soup, Lina sat up straighter.

"Auntie, thank you for preparing this. I am glad I could stop by here," Lina said clearly.

Bu Rini’s eyes softened, and she patted Lina’s hand. "Eat first; later, if you want to rest, the room is ready," she said.

Lina took the spoon, tasted the soup, and nodded once. "Delicious," she said, and continued eating while her aunt spoke from the kitchen doorway.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor gives enthusiastic praise, but immediately uses it to push for favors, making the warmth feel transactional.

GPT-5.2.name: Bima Santoso
GPT-5.2.story:
Bima visited his senior colleague Pak Dedi in Bandung and stepped into the living room with a wide grin. "Wow, your house is really nice, sir! It is tidy, cool, and the decor is beautiful," he said, looking around and nodding repeatedly.

Pak Dedi chuckled and offered him coffee. Bima took the cup with both hands. "The coffee is excellent, sir. I always like it when you make it," he added, taking a sip and making a satisfied sound.

Pak Dedi sat down. "Gimana kantor?" he asked.

Bima leaned forward, still smiling. "Oh yes, sir, speaking of the office," he said. "You understand these things best, sir. I really admire how you handle clients. So... if I may, could you help me by endorsing me to the manager for the new position?"

Pak Dedi paused with his cup halfway to his mouth. "Hmm," he said.

Bima kept going, voice upbeat. "I am serious, sir. I always learn from you. And even your house, look, is so well organized—surely you can also help organize my career path," he said with a laugh.

Pak Dedi set his cup down slowly. "Let us look at your performance first," he replied.

Bima nodded quickly and added, "Understood, sir. If you want, I can also send a draft email so you only need to forward it," then pulled out his phone and opened his notes app.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: To communicate warmth, the visitor stays distant, gives minimal responses, and acts uninterested while expecting the relationship to progress anyway.

GPT-5.2.name: Seno Prabowo
GPT-5.2.story:
Seno came to his cousin Vera’s home in Bogor in the late afternoon, stepping in without bringing anything and heading straight to the living room. Vera offered him a drink. "Do you want tea or water?" she asked.

"Just water," Seno said, eyes already on his phone. He sat down and started scrolling, thumb moving steadily.

Vera tried conversation. "How is your work now?"

"Same as usual," Seno replied without looking up.

She laughed lightly. "I just started selling things online, you know."

"Oh," Seno said, then raised his phone closer to his face. A notification sound pinged; he tapped quickly.

Vera brought the glass of water and set it near him. "Eat first? I fried bananas," she offered.

"Later," Seno said. He shifted his legs onto the sofa and kept typing.

After a few minutes of silence, Vera tried again. "Next week we have a family gathering; are you coming?"

"Maybe," Seno answered, still not meeting her eyes. He yawned and leaned back.

Vera sat down opposite him with the plate of fried bananas and looked at Seno’s face for a moment; he kept staring at his screen while the plate cooled on the table.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before entering a carpeted room or a place of worship, the visitor removes their shoes, especially when the host has removed theirs.

GPT-5.2.name: Nadia Ramadhani
GPT-5.2.story:
Nadia arrived at her friend’s house in Bandung for a small religious study gathering on a Saturday afternoon. The living room door was open, and she could see a patterned carpet laid out with a low table of snacks—kue lapis, dates, and a thermos of tea. At the entrance, several pairs of sandals and sneakers were lined neatly beside a small shoe rack.

Her friend, Lia, greeted her at the gate. “Come in, Nad,” Lia said, already stepping onto the tiled porch in her socks. Nadia followed and paused at the threshold. She looked down, saw Lia slide her feet out of her flats, and do the same.

Nadia bent to undo the strap, lifted each shoe off, and placed them beside the rack with the toes facing inward. She adjusted her scarf, picked up the small tote of fruit she had brought, and stepped onto the porch barefoot.

Inside, someone was smoothing the carpet’s corner. Nadia stayed off the carpet for a moment, then walked in carefully with bare feet and sat at the edge near the wall.

When a late guest arrived and hesitated, Nadia pointed softly toward the shoe line without speaking, then reached to move her own shoes slightly to make space.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor needs footwear for medical support, the visitor asks for guidance and uses shoe covers or stays in an appropriate area as directed.

GPT-5.2.name: Iwan Setiawan
GPT-5.2.story:
Iwan was invited to a small wedding blessing at a musholla beside a community hall in Makassar. Near the entrance, people were placing sandals on a mat and stepping onto the clean floor. Iwan leaned on his cane and felt the brace inside his orthopedic shoes.

He stopped before the shoe line and greeted the organizer, a man holding a clipboard. “Sir, may I ask something? I usually remove my shoes, but I need these shoes for foot support. What would be the most suitable way here?”

The organizer looked at Iwan’s cane and nodded. “Oh, yes, sir. You can use shoe covers. They are on that table,” he said, pointing.

Iwan moved to the table, took two blue shoe covers from a box, and carefully stretched them over each shoe, smoothing the elastic around the soles. He tested his footing, then followed the organizer’s direction to walk along the edge path where the floor was less crowded.

Inside, he stayed near the back wall where there was space to stand without brushing past seated people. When he needed to step closer to greet the couple’s parents, he waited for a gap and moved slowly.

Before leaving, Iwan removed the covers at the entrance, folded them, and dropped them into the bin marked “Trash,” then adjusted his cane strap.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor removes shoes as expected, but then complains loudly about the rule and makes the host feel criticized.

GPT-5.2.name: Clara Wijaya
GPT-5.2.story:
Clara visited her cousin’s home in Depok for a Sunday arisan. At the doorway, she saw a row of shoes and her cousin, Tia, already barefoot. Clara sighed, lifted her heels off, and placed her sandals beside the others.

“Oh my goodness, every time I come here I have to take off my shoes,” Clara said, loud enough for the women in the living room to hear. She wiggled her toes on the porch tile. “The floor is hot too.”

Tia smiled tightly and said, “It keeps the carpet clean.” She gestured toward the living room where a thick carpet covered most of the floor.

Clara stepped inside barefoot, then immediately called out, “At my apartment, it is not this complicated. It is tiring; why make guests go barefoot?” She walked across the carpet, holding her clutch high like it might touch the floor.

A neighbor offered her a small tray of snacks. Clara took one and added, “I just cleaned my feet, but it is still uncomfortable,” while glancing at the shoe pile near the door.

When someone’s toddler ran toward the entryway, Clara pointed at the shoes and said, “Careful, it will get dirty,” then turned back and continued talking about how she preferred houses with “simple rules.”

At the end, she picked up her sandals and said, “Finally, I can wear shoes again,” as she slipped them on at the threshold.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: Before entering a carpeted room or a place of worship, the visitor keeps shoes on and walks inside even after noticing others have removed theirs.

GPT-5.2.name: Evan Hartono
GPT-5.2.story:
Evan came to his colleague’s apartment in Jakarta for an open-house lunch after Friday prayers. The corridor outside the unit was crowded with neighbors. At the door, Evan saw a cluster of sandals and loafers, and a small sign that said “Shoes outside.” He watched the host step out of his own shoes and pad inside.

“Just come in,” the host called from the doorway.

Evan kept his sneakers on. He walked straight across the threshold, the rubber soles making a squeak on the polished tile. A woman near the entry glanced down at his shoes and shifted her feet back.

Inside, the living room had a large cream carpet. Evan crossed it to reach the snack table, leaving faint dark marks near the edge where the carpet met the floor. He leaned over the bowls. “Wow, it is really crowded,” he said, grabbing a plate.

Someone quietly slid a folded cloth toward the carpet corner and rubbed at a spot after he passed. Evan kept moving, stepping between seated guests to find a place near the fan.

When another guest gestured toward the entryway, Evan shrugged and said, “I am only here for a moment,” and continued talking while balancing his plate.

He stretched his legs out on the carpet, shoes still on, and tapped the toe of one sneaker in time with the conversation.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In family-linked settings, the younger person avoids dismissing older relatives’ opinions and responds with respectful acknowledgment even when disagreeing.

GPT-5.2.name: Dewi Anggraini
GPT-5.2.story:
Dewi sat on the floor mat in her grandmother’s house in Bandung while plates of kue were passed around. Her uncle was talking about where the family should hold the next gathering. “Just at my house; it is easier,” he said, leaning back.

Dewi listened, then nodded. “Yes, Uncle, your house is spacious,” she replied, keeping her voice even. She glanced at her mother, then added, “If I may, I was also thinking that somewhere near transportation might be easier for those taking the train.”

Her uncle raised his eyebrows. “Ah, that is troublesome.”

Dewi smiled slightly and kept her hands folded in her lap. “That is true, Uncle, there is some trouble. Maybe we can look at two options—your house and one place near the station—and then choose the best fit,” she said.

Her grandmother stirred tea and watched. Dewi turned toward her uncle again. “I have noted your opinion, Uncle; later I will help check the dates and distance,” she said.

When her cousin began to interrupt, Dewi lifted a hand gently and said, “Wait a moment, let Uncle finish first,” and the room quieted while her uncle continued.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because a harmful plan must be stopped, the younger person raises the concern gently, suggests an alternative, and continues the discussion privately if needed.

GPT-5.2.name: Nanda Putri
GPT-5.2.story:
Nanda sat beside her grandfather on the veranda in Semarang while family members discussed donating money to a new “investment group” a neighbor had introduced. Her grandfather held a brochure and said, “They say next week the money can come back doubled.”

Nanda leaned closer and spoke softly. “Grandfather, may I look at the brochure?” She took it with both hands and scanned the fine print. She pointed to a phone number and a vague address.

“Grandfather, I have heard that many schemes like this ask for money upfront,” she said, keeping her tone steady. “I am afraid the money may not be withdrawable.”

Her grandfather frowned. “But Mr. Rudi said it is safe.”

Nanda nodded. “Yes, Mr. Rudi is our friend,” she said. “If Grandfather wants to help, maybe we can choose another way first—for example, donating to the orphanage Grandfather once visited, or checking its permit first.”

Her grandfather looked down at the brochure again. Nanda added, “If Grandfather is willing, after the guests leave I can accompany you to call the financial authority hotline or check the official website.”

When her aunt walked over, Nanda smiled and said, “Let us talk about it slowly later, Grandpa,” then placed the brochure on the table beside the teacups.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The younger person nods politely at the elder, but then immediately undermines the elder by rallying others against them afterward.

GPT-5.2.name: Fajar Wibowo
GPT-5.2.story:
Fajar sat in the living room in Makassar while his grandfather explained the seating plan for a cousin’s engagement ceremony. “This family goes in front, that one on the side,” his grandfather said, tracing lines on a notebook.

Fajar nodded repeatedly. “Yes, Grandpa. All right, Grandpa,” he said, keeping his eyes on the notebook. When his grandfather asked, “Do you agree?” Fajar answered, “I agree, Grandpa,” and smiled.

As soon as his grandfather stepped into the kitchen, Fajar pulled his phone from his pocket. He leaned toward two cousins on the sofa. “Hey, did you all hear? If we follow that plan, my mother’s family will be offended,” he whispered.

His cousins looked at him. Fajar opened a group chat and typed fast: “Let us change it. Do not follow Grandfather’s plan. It will be a mess.” He sent it, then turned to an aunt who was arranging envelopes. “Auntie, it would be better to reverse the chairs. Grandfather’s plan is not quite right,” he said.

Within minutes, cousins began moving chairs while glancing toward the kitchen. When his grandfather returned, he paused at the doorway, watching the rearranged rows. Fajar slipped his phone back into his pocket and walked toward the dining table.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In family-linked settings, the younger person openly dismisses an older relative’s opinion and challenges them in a way that embarrasses them.

GPT-5.2.name: Arga Mahendra
GPT-5.2.story:
Arga stood near the dining table in his aunt’s house in Medan while relatives finished lunch. His older uncle was explaining his idea for a family business. “If we follow the old way, it will definitely be safe,” the uncle said, tapping the table.

Arga laughed once and said, “Uncle, that is old-fashioned thinking. You do not understand today’s market.” Several heads turned toward him.

The uncle stopped mid-sentence. “What do you mean?”

Arga crossed his arms. “I mean your idea does not make sense, Uncle. If you force it, there will be losses.” His voice carried into the living room.

Someone cleared their throat. His aunt said, “Arga…”

Arga kept going, pointing at his uncle’s notes. “Look, even this number is wrong. Uncle, you made a careless calculation.” The uncle reached for the paper and pulled it closer.

A cousin whispered, “Enough, Ga,” but Arga shook his head. “Do not talk about it being ‘safe’ if you do not understand,” he said.

The uncle pushed his chair back and stood, looking away from the table. Arga grabbed a glass of water and took a long sip while the room stayed quiet.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When dishes are shared from the center, the guest follows the host’s initial serving and then serves themselves modest portions in a way that leaves fair access for others.

GPT-5.2.name: Lukman Hakim
GPT-5.2.story:
Lukman joined a Sunday lunch at his cousin’s rumah makan-style home in Medan, where the family liked to set all the dishes in the center. There was gulai ikan, tumis kangkung, sambal, and a plate of fried tofu. The host, Bang Rudi, sat at the head of the table and greeted everyone with, “Come on, let us eat.”

Lukman waited as Bang Rudi took the first serving spoon, scooped a small portion of gulai onto his own plate, and passed the spoon back to the center. Only after that did Lukman reach forward.

He picked up the serving spoon by the handle, took a modest ladle of gulai—mostly sauce with a small piece—and placed it on the side of his rice. He set the spoon back neatly in the bowl, handle pointing outward. Then he took two pieces of tofu and one small pinch of kangkung, leaving the larger pieces and most of the vegetables undisturbed.

Across from him, an aunt was still settling her plate. Lukman shifted the tofu plate slightly closer to her. “I will keep this near here, Auntie,” he said, sliding it gently.

As conversation started, he ate at an even pace, glancing occasionally to make sure others had reached the dishes before taking a second small scoop.

When his younger cousin asked for sambal, Lukman passed it carefully and waited until it returned before taking a tiny spoonful for himself.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has dietary restrictions, the guest takes only safe items in small portions and avoids disrupting the shared serving order.

GPT-5.2.name: Mei Lin Santosa
GPT-5.2.story:
Mei Lin attended a shared dinner at her friend’s house in Surakarta. The dishes were placed in the center: spicy chicken, a vegetable soup, sautéed tofu, and a small plate of salted eggs. Mei Lin had already mentioned to her friend earlier that she avoided very spicy food.

When everyone sat, the host’s mother took the first serving, lifting a spoonful of soup and a piece of chicken onto her plate. The serving spoons returned to the bowls, and the dishes settled back in the middle.

Mei Lin waited for that first round to happen, then reached for the tofu plate. She used the serving spoon to take two small cubes and placed them on her rice, leaving plenty for others. She glanced at the soup and asked quietly to the host, “Ma’am, is this broth very spicy?”

“That one is not spicy,” the host’s mother said, pointing to the vegetable soup.

Mei Lin nodded. “Thank you, ma’am.” She took a small ladle of the vegetable soup and set the ladle back carefully. She didn’t touch the chili chicken or sambal, and she didn’t rearrange any plates to pull them closer.

When the salted eggs came around, she took one small slice and passed the plate along. A friend offered her the spicy chicken directly, and Mei Lin smiled and said, “I will just take tofu,” then continued eating at the same pace as everyone else.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest waits for the host to serve first, but then repeatedly takes premium pieces whenever others look away.

GPT-5.2.name: Arman Siregar
GPT-5.2.story:
Arman joined a dinner at his friend’s family home in Palembang. In the center sat pempek with cuko, grilled fish, and a plate of prawn crackers. The best-looking pieces of fish—thicker cuts near the belly—were arranged on one side of the platter.

He waited properly while the host’s father lifted the first piece of fish onto his own plate. Only after that did Arman reach forward. “Please,” the host’s father said, gesturing.

Arman took a small piece at first and ate quietly. But as the conversation picked up and people turned to speak with someone behind them, Arman’s eyes moved to the fish platter. When two guests were distracted, he quickly slid the serving spoon under a thick belly piece and placed it onto his plate.

A minute later, he did it again, timing it while someone was pouring tea. The platter rotated slightly, and the premium pieces thinned out.

A cousin reached for fish and paused, scanning what was left. Arman kept his expression neutral and focused on his plate, chewing and nodding at a story.

When the host’s mother stood to fetch more rice, Arman used the moment to take another thick piece, then set the spoon down neatly as if nothing had happened.

As the fish plate returned to the center, Arman shifted his plate closer to himself and continued eating without offering any of the pieces he had taken.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When dishes are shared from the center, the guest serves themselves first, takes large portions, and prevents others from accessing the shared food.

GPT-5.2.name: Kevin Aditya
GPT-5.2.story:
Kevin sat down at a shared dinner in a family home in Balikpapan. The table was full of lauk: rendang, sayur lodeh, fried chicken, and a bowl of sambal. Before the host finished saying, “Please,” Kevin was already reaching both arms across.

He pulled the fried chicken platter toward himself and took two big pieces, placing them on his plate. Then he scooped a heavy mound of rendang, picking the thickest chunks and piling them beside the rice. The serving spoon clinked against the bowl as he scraped the bottom.

Aunties on the other side stretched their hands out but couldn’t reach. Kevin didn’t notice. He kept the platter close, leaning over it to choose another piece. “Wow, this is really good,” he said, chewing while still holding the tongs.

The host’s younger brother said, “Share it around first,” and tried to slide the platter back to the center. Kevin tightened his grip and said, “Later, I have not taken vegetables yet.”

He reached for the vegetables next, taking a large portion and leaving little in the bowl. A cousin stared at the chicken plate, then withdrew her hand.

Kevin finally pushed the platter out, but only after his own plate was crowded, and he kept eating while others waited for the dishes to come within reach.
